commentary_text: The model rates Mexico to win at 82% while the market offers 64%, a 18-percentage-point gap favoring the model's confidence in Mexico. The market simultaneously assigns South Africa a 14% win probability versus the model's 1%, suggesting sportsbooks are pricing in material uncertainty the model does not capture. Recent form, lineup news, or injury developments affecting either team could explain why the market is hedging its Mexico conviction—the model's last update may predate shifts in squad availability or momentum that real-money flows now reflect. The dashboard flags this divergence as a signal for monitoring; neither source has proven authority over the other.
